title: Sidebar
description: Foldkit-native shadcn sidebar shell built from local shell, menu, tooltip, collapsible, sheet, avatar, and input primitives with explicit parent-controlled state.
markdown:
# Sidebar

## Overview

Sidebar is a Foldkit-native shadcn shell for app navigation. It composes the
local Sheet, Collapsible, Dropdown Menu, Tooltip, Avatar, Input, Separator,
Skeleton, Button, and Direction registry items so the installable source stays
Effect- and Foldkit-native while preserving the origin surface.

## Foldkit Model

Sidebar is a controlled shell composition. The parent model owns the facts for
desktop open state, collapsed state, mobile sheet state, active item state, and
direction. There is no hidden React context, cookie-backed persistence, media
query hook, or ambient keyboard listener in the installable source.

## Usage

Bind `const h = html<Message>()` in the parent view and pass explicit sidebar
state into the shell helpers.

```ts
Sidebar.SidebarProvider<Message>({
  dir: model.direction,
  children: [
    Sidebar.Sidebar<Message>({
      id: 'app-sidebar',
      open: model.open,
      mobileOpen: model.mobileOpen,
      isMobile: model.isMobile,
      collapsible: 'offcanvas',
      children: [...],
    }),
    Sidebar.SidebarInset<Message>({
      children: [...],
    }),
  ],
})
```

Use the message layer to express user intent such as toggling the sidebar,
opening the mobile sheet, or selecting a navigation item. Keep persistence and
keyboard shortcuts explicit in the parent update function.

## Examples

The registry includes demo, controlled, footer, group action, group collapsible,
header, menu action, menu badge, menu collapsible, menu sub, menu, RSC, and RTL
examples. The examples cover the shell, grouped navigation, mobile sheet branch,
submenu composition, account menu composition, badges, and direction-aware
layout.

## Accessibility

The shell preserves button, link, list, and dialog semantics through local
Foldkit primitives. Use visible text for navigation items, keep the trigger and
rail buttons labeled, and provide descriptive submenu labels so collapsed or
mobile layouts still remain usable.

## Foldkit Differences

The origin shadcn sidebar relies on React context, a `useMobile` hook, cookies,
and a global keyboard shortcut. This registry item replaces those pieces with an
explicit parent-controlled model, local Foldkit primitives, and local class
helpers.

Those differences are intentional. They keep the installable source aligned with
Foldkit architecture while preserving the origin visual contract and the
supported example set.

registry.reason: Sidebar footer and demo examples compose the local Avatar wrapper.
Sidebar trigger and controlled example actions compose the local Button wrapper.
Sidebar group and submenu examples use the local Collapsible wrapper.
Sidebar examples include explicit RTL composition and direction-aware class helpers.
Sidebar footer and action examples use the local Dropdown Menu wrapper.
Sidebar header examples use the local Input wrapper for search.
Sidebar uses the local Separator wrapper for section dividers.
Sidebar mobile branch composes the local Sheet wrapper.
Sidebar menu skeleton helper composes the local Skeleton wrapper.
Sidebar demo examples include tooltip composition for collapsed-style affordances.
Sidebar class helpers use the local cn canonicalizer.

runtime.reason: Provides Html and html factory APIs for the Foldkit-native wrapper.
Provides Effect Schema literals for sidebar variants, states, and style options.
Provided through the local utils/cn registry dependency.
Provided through the local utils/cn registry dependency.

development.reason: Origin sidebar source and fixtures are React-based; installable source stays Foldkit-native.
Origin sidebar examples use lucide-react icons; installable source uses local inline SVGs.
Origin React helper is replaced by local Foldkit composition.
Origin React helper is replaced by local Foldkit composition.
Origin sidebar CVA variant handling is replaced by explicit Schema literals and class maps.
Sidebar mobility is represented explicitly in the parent model instead of a hidden media-query hook.

deviations.summary: Open, collapsed, mobile, and active item state are explicit parent-controlled facts instead of a hidden React context or cookie-backed provider.
deviations.rationale: Foldkit keeps state in the model and avoids hidden browser storage or ambient React context.

deviations.summary: The installable source does not install a hidden global keyboard listener for cmd/ctrl+b.
deviations.rationale: Global listeners hidden in view code are forbidden here. Keyboard shortcut facts must be explicit model or command boundary events.
